objective: Rewrite Sage from a tool-protocol-first LangGraph loop to a plain-text-first, editable-response-session UX with backend-only tool execution and end-to-end legacy cleanup.

milestones.title: Capture intake snapshot and touched scope
notes: Work item scaffolded from the local workflow generator.

milestones.title: Implement the scoped changes
notes: Rewrote the LangGraph runtime, prompt contract, and Discord transport around a plain-text-first response-session model.

milestones.title: Run validation and publish the completion report
notes: Repository validation and docs gates passed after the rewrite, and the matching completion report was written.

progress.summary: Created the local work item scaffold.
Completed the plain-text-first rewrite, updated the docs/tests, and finished the validation/reporting pass.

decision_log.decision: Remove the terminal closeout tool contract and restore no-tool assistant turns as the finalization condition.
decision_log.rationale: The plain-text-first rewrite requires assistant prose to be the primary user-facing surface, so terminal delivery tools would keep the old architecture alive.

decision_log.decision: Implement response-session message editing in the Discord transport by consuming streamed graph state updates.
decision_log.rationale: This keeps message send/edit side effects in the transport layer and avoids reintroducing tool-delivery logic into the core runtime.

discoveries.summary: The existing Discord message handler could support in-place edits by consuming streamed graph state without a separate transport subsystem rewrite.
evidence: runChatTurn already streams graph state transitions, so the handler could create and edit one response message from callback data alone.

discoveries.summary: Most of the protocol rigidity lived in the runtime and prompt layers, so removing it did not require a provider-client redesign.
evidence: The provider client already preserved assistant content and tool_calls together; the invalidation behavior was enforced higher in the stack.

validation_strategy: Run lint, unit tests, TypeScript build, and tracked docs checks after the rewrite.
Validate local workflow artifacts and audit the canonical workflow layout before closing the task.
notes: This run intentionally keeps response-session continuity adapter-local for the current execution; persisted cross-run response message binding was not added in this pass.
